shelfmark: Bodl. MS heb. f 56/4 + Bodl. MS heb. f 56/5
description: Chancery or fiscal document, presumably Mamluk period. There are two lines preserved, consisting of a date (22nd Rajab is visible) and the ḥamdala. The text was folded down the middle to form a bifolio and reused for notes on the Jewish calendar, a roster for the years starting with 1275/6 until the mid-fourteenth century. But the roster contains errors, such as parts of the sequence that are repeated. There are also jottings, including a biblical quotation. (Marina Rustow, with information on the calendar roster from Sacha Stern)
description_he: מסמך ממשלתי/פיסקאלי, ייתכן מהתקופה הממלוכית. שמורות שתי שורות: תאריך (22 רג'ב) וחמדלה. שימוש חוזר ללוח שנה יהודי ממשנת 1275/6 עד אמצע המאה ה-14.
